summary: Tested NEW admin user-management feature end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 pytest tests passed (list/create/delete admins, role guards 401/403, duplicate email 400, short password 422, self-delete 400, non-existent 404, non-admin user 404, full create-then-login flow). Frontend: /admin/admins page renders, Admins nav link visible, Add Admin dialog creates admin and table updates immediately, delete disabled for self (you badge), delete works for other admin, student is redirected from /admin/admins to /dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: Last-admin guard at DELETE /api/admin/admins/{user_id} (count<=1 check) is logically unreachable: to call DELETE the caller must be admin, and self-deletion is blocked first; therefore total_admins>=2 is always true at the count check, and target!=self means total>=2 admins remain. The guard is harmless but can never fire. Consider documenting or removing.
POST /api/admin/admins does not call set_auth_cookie or otherwise log the new admin in; that is correct per spec (admin creates account on behalf of new admin) — flag only to confirm intent.

AdminAdmins.jsx isLast = admins.length <= 1 disables delete on the only admin row, but since the admin is also self in that case, the disabled state is already covered by isYou. Minor redundancy, no bug.
Admin password is sent as type='text' in the create dialog (apass input). Intentional per spec ('share securely with new admin'), but consider toggle to show/hide.
No audit log of who created/removed which admin — consider logging for security feature.
Pre-existing notes from iteration 1 still apply: no brute-force lockout on /api/auth/login; CORS allow_credentials=False so cookie auth is dead path — relies entirely on Bearer.

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 new admin-mgmt tests; 18/18 prior suite untouched)
success_rate.frontend: 100% (login -> admins page -> create -> delete; self-disabled; student redirect all verified)

seed_data_creation: Each test run creates ephemeral admin users (newadmin.*, adminA.*, adminB.*, target.*, uitest.admin.*) and deletes them. The success-flow test deletes both extras it creates so DB pollution is minimal. A few extra admins may remain from the create-and-login test (intentionally not deleted) — clean up via Mongo if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin user-management feature is fully functional: GET/POST/DELETE /api/admin/admins all pass with proper role guards. Frontend page at /admin/admins works: data-testid attributes ('new-admin-button', 'admin-name-input', 'admin-email-input', 'admin-password-input', 'admin-submit-button', 'delete-admin-{id}', 'admin-row-{id}') are all present and reliable. Confirm dialog (window.confirm) is used for delete - use page.on('dialog', d.accept()) in playwright. Students are correctly redirected from /admin/* via Protected component to /dashboard. Note: when navigating in admin nav after login, the success toast can intercept clicks for ~2s; either wait_for_timeout(2500) or use page.goto() directly. Last-admin guard cannot be exercised in practice (see code review note).
rca of the issue: n/a — no bugs found in the new feature.
